title: Taiwan MND Detects 6 PLA Aircraft Sorties, 12 PLAN Vessels
detail: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected 6 sorties of PLA aircraft, 10 PLAN vessels, and 2 official ships operating around Taiwan on March 27. Four of the 6 sorties crossed the median line and entered Taiwan's southwestern and eastern ADIZ. ROC Armed Forces deployed CAP aircraft, Navy ships, and coastal missile systems. Activity returned to moderate levels after the March 25 single-day spike of 16 sorties (13 crossing the median line). Analysts note monthly totals are tracking significantly below 2024 averages, consistent with PLA signaling adjustment ahead of the rescheduled Trump-Xi summit.

title: US Envoy Urges Taiwan Legislature to Pass NT$1.25T Special Defense Budget
detail: A US envoy publicly called on Taiwan's legislators to pass the NT$1.25 trillion (approximately $38 billion) 8-year special defense budget bill as committee deliberations stalled in the week of March 22–27. Lawmakers failed to agree on most provisions — only 2 of many articles passed in committee. The opposition KMT proposes NT$380 billion and the TPP proposes NT$400 billion, far below the Lai administration's bill. The bill covers US-made systems (HIMARS, PAC-3, NASAMS), domestic drone programs, and the T-Dome air defense network. The legislative impasse undermines Taiwan's ability to lock in pending US arms packages, including the $14 billion PAC-3/NASAMS package awaiting President Trump's approval after his postponed Beijing summit.
